report_name: Canada Warns Connected Vehicle Data Has 'Intelligence Value' for Foreign Adversaries

threat_actors.name: Foreign Adversaries
threat_actors.description: State actors from adversarial nations, such as China, identified by Public Safety Canada as having an interest in collecting data from connected vehicles for intelligence purposes, including surveillance and tracking of Canadian citizens.
threat_actors.goals: Large-scale surveillance or tracking of Canadian citizens, and intelligence gathering on a national scale.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: Foreign adversaries could access vehicle data transmitted to and stored in countries with permissive data management frameworks, treating the foreign storage as an information repository for intelligence gathering.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Exfiltration
attack_patterns.description: Connected vehicles automatically exfiltrate large volumes of telematics, location, and sensor data to manufacturer servers, which could be located in adversarial nations, enabling automated intelligence collection.

attack_patterns.name: Gather Victim Org Information
attack_patterns.description: Vehicle data from government and military personnel could be used to gather organizational information by exposing sensitive locations, routines, and patterns of life.

tools.name: Artificial Intelligence
tools.description: Mentioned as a technology that could enhance the capabilities of malicious actors to process vast amounts of collected vehicle data for intelligence purposes.

identities.name: Public Safety Canada
identities.description: Canadian government agency that authored an internal memo warning about the national security risks associated with data collection by connected vehicles.

identities.name: Transport Canada
identities.description: Canadian government agency collaborating with international partners to develop global security standards for automated driving systems.

identities.name: United Nations
identities.description: International body working with Transport Canada to develop global regulations for the cybersecurity of automated driving systems.

identities.name: The Canadian Public
identities.description: The general population of Canada, identified as potential targets for large-scale surveillance through data collected by their connected vehicles.

identities.name: Government and Military Personnel
identities.description: A high-value group of individuals targeted for surveillance, whose vehicle data could expose sensitive locations and routines.

identities.name: Automotive Industry
identities.description: Vehicle manufacturers, especially foreign ones, who may face increased scrutiny and future regulatory requirements regarding data handling and security.

infrastructure.name: Connected Vehicle Data Collection Platform
infrastructure.description: Modern connected vehicles are described as sophisticated, mobile data collection platforms equipped with GPS, cameras, microphones, and telematics systems used to harvest data.

infrastructure.name: Foreign Data Storage Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Data storage infrastructure located in countries with permissive data management frameworks, where vehicle data could be exfiltrated to and accessed by state actors.

indicators.name: Anomalous Vehicle Telematics Data Exfiltration
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Vehicle Telematics Data Exfiltration to Foreign Nations
id: 2b1d8c9a-4f5e-4b6d-8c7a-9f8e7d6c5b4a
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects potentially large-scale exfiltration of telematics data from connected vehicles to servers in countries considered adversarial or having permissive data management frameworks, as highlighted by a Public Safety Canada memo. This activity could indicate foreign intelligence gathering.
references:
  - https://toronto.citynews.ca/2026/06/01/connected-vehicle-data-can-have-intelligence-value-to-adversaries-federal-document/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1020
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate data transfer for vehicle services, updates, or diagnostics to servers located in these regions.
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    # This is a conceptual rule. Specific IPs/domains for countries like China would be needed.
    # The rule detects traffic from a known telematics source IP to a destination in a country of concern.
    source_ip_class:
      - 'telematics_unit_ip_range'
    destination_country:
      - 'CN'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects large volumes of telematics data being exfiltrated from connected vehicles to servers in foreign countries, a risk highlighted by Public Safety Canada.
